name: g15_transport_emergence_stack
purpose: Define where faces and chirality emerge across the G15, shell, G30, and G60 layers so the project keeps a stable ontology.
core_claim: Faces emerge at projection. Chirality emerges at transport.

layers.name: G15_kernel
layers.statement: The G15 kernel gives the exact adjacency and transport combinatorics, but does not by itself force the shell face interpretation.

layers.name: shell_projection
layers.statement: Faces become legible when the lifted state is projected into the shell as sides, regions, and inverse-face structure.

layers.name: G30_lift
layers.statement: Chirality becomes lawful when sheet memory is retained across traversal, so transport distinguishes present face from inverse face.

layers.name: G60_host
layers.statement: G60 does not invent new chirality; it orders face and chirality events across the full host cycle.

face_definition.statement: A face is a projected side of the lifted transport state.

chirality_definition.statement: Chirality is the retained memory of how transport crosses between sides.

minimal_rules: Do not claim that bare G15 adjacency already fixes the face ontology.
Do not treat chirality as mere styling.
Do not define chirality before the transport law retains sheet memory.
Do not treat G60 as a source of new local chirality; it is a chronology layer.

bridge_to_existing_specs.theorem_object: anchors the exact kernel object M and Q
bridge_to_existing_specs.transport_state_machine: anchors the lifted G30 transport law
bridge_to_existing_specs.transport_host_state_machine: anchors the G60 chronology of the lifted law
bridge_to_existing_specs.transport_cocycle: anchors the overlap and loop-memory transition law
